story.title: Dr. Tran and the Underground Water Puzzle
story.text:
My name is Dr. Kevin Tran. I am a groundwater hydrologist. I study water that is hidden underground.

This underground water is called groundwater. It is stored in a special layer of rock and sand. This layer is called an aquifer. I help communities in California understand their water supply.

I use a computer model to see the whole water system. My model shows how rain fills the aquifer. More rainfall means more water goes underground. This is a positive relationship.

My model also looks at the number of people. A bigger population uses more water for homes and farms. This increases the water usage rate. This is also a positive relationship.

But here is the challenge. A high water usage rate pumps water out of the aquifer. This makes the groundwater level go down. This is a negative relationship.

When groundwater levels are low, there is less water for everyone. This reduces water availability. My job is to help communities plan. We must work together to save water for our future.
think_abouts: What is the connection between the number of people and the amount of water?
Why is it important for Dr. Tran to predict when a community might run out of water?
story.go_deeper: Dr. Tran also studies farming. Some farms waste 50% of their water. How could using better farming tools help a community's water availability?

margin_defs.definition: a simple way to show something complex

check_prompts: What happens to the groundwater level when the water usage rate goes up?

vocabulary.definition: Water stored underground in soil and rock layers.
vocabulary.use_sentence: We use a well to pump groundwater to our house.

vocabulary.definition: An underground layer of rock or sediment that holds water.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The deep aquifer provides water for the whole city.

vocabulary.definition: How fast a community uses its available water supply.
vocabulary.use_sentence: In the summer, our water usage rate is very high.

vocabulary.definition: Whether there is enough clean water for everyone in a community.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A long drought can lower water availability.

discussion_cards.prompt: What are two reasons a community's groundwater level might go down?
discussion_cards.frame: A community's groundwater level might go down because of ___ and ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: If you were the leader of a town with low water availability, what is one new rule you would make to save water? Why?
discussion_cards.frame: One new rule I would make is ___ because ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
